operation: First, initialize a workplane on the XY plane, draw a regular 6-sided polygon with a diameter of 18 units, and extrude the sketch to a height of 12 units to form a hexagonal prism. Next, create a new workplane on the top (highest Z) face of the prism, then add a concentric cylinder with a height of 8 units and radius of 7 units. Finally, select all edges parallel to the Z axis (the vertical edges of the hexagonal prism) and apply a 2.5 unit chamfer to each of these edges.
geometry: The model is a single solid consisting of two concentric components sharing a common central vertical axis: a lower regular hexagonal prism, and an upper right circular cylinder positioned centered on the top surface of the prism. All six vertical corner edges of the lower hexagonal prism are chamfered, creating small angled flat faces at each vertical corner of the faceted lower section.
function: This is a manual grip knob intended for adjustment, control, or fastening use. Its faceted hexagonal lower section provides improved traction for hand turning, chamfered edges remove sharp points for safer handling, and the smooth cylindrical top forms a flat, unobstructed upper surface.
